content: When you send messages to yourself, is it received? If the account is hosted by a web host, the error may be security issues causing the messages to be failing. I suggest working with your email host on this. There are a number of server settings that are unreachable by Thunderbird that can only be set by accessing the account on the server.

automatic updates Some settings appear to be incorrect. Did you have automatic updates enabled? Updates often alter settings for extensions or add-ons. An automatic update may have altered your dark mode or other extensions, affecting their colors. Please check all extensions and add-ons to verify if they have updated.

content:
The out-of-the-box Print function reflects your View | Headers setting, that is, if you're set to Normal you will print minimal headers only, if you're set to "All" you'll get much more. As far as I know, that's all the control you have without the aid of an add-on. Check out "PrintingTools NG" which gives you many more options.

https://services.addons.thunderbird.net/en-US/thunderbird/addon/printingtools-ng/

content:
Error code 550 5.7.1 indicates that your message has been rejected by the receiver's mail system. That can happen for a variety of reasons, including that your email service is on a block list, or is misconfigured. You can read a more technical description here:

https://email-knowledge.com/bouncecodes/5-7-1/

Unless you have direct control of your mail server settings, there is nothing your can do about it except to confirm your server settings and contact your email service tech support. It's not a Thunderbird thing.
creator: rednoise

content: Thunderbird uses a filter that the user must train. The" https://blog.thunderbird.net/2024/09/thunderbird-and-spam/ You might also check with your email host, as they sometimes offer whitelists and blacklists. On a personal level, I also use Mailwasher (it;s free for one account) to keep spam out.

content: It is very possible to switch email programs. What is the new one? What was the old one? Was it an older version of Thunderbird or some other program?
creator: rednoise

content: People sometimes perceive a new version of Thunderbird to be very different, and then they learn the layout settings that make the new version look almost exactly like the old version. If you describe what you liked about the old version and what you don't like about the new version, we can tell you what to do. That will be easier and better for you than replacing a new version with an old one.
creator: mozilla98

content:
Per poter inquadrare il problema prova a fornire queste informazioni:

  1. Versione di Thunderbird
  2. Versione del sistema operativo
  3. Provider delle caselle email (Gmail, Libero, Hotmail, etc.)
  4. Tipologia di account (POP3, IMAP)
  5. Estensioni installate
  6. Messaggi di errore che vengono visualizzati durante l'utilizzo di Thunderbird
  7. Software di sicurezza eventualmente presenti (Antivirus e simili)
  8. Screenshot del problema (se utile a chiarire il contesto)
creator: @next
